item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: i love these, they're perfect

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The images used in this article have been replaced, as the original images were non-compliant with the SCP Wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). They are sourced as follows:

+ Show attribution
- Hide attribution

Filename: clownscpsite-new.jpg
Author: Elogee FishTruck, Famartin
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Source Link: SCP Foundation Wiki
Derivative of:

Name: 2015-05-03 18 35 37 The Clown Motel on Main Street (U.S. Route 6 and 95) in Tonopah, Nevada.jpg
Author: Famartin
License: CC BY-SA 4.0
Source Link: Wikimedia Commons

Filename: clown-mask-new.png
Name: Clown face vector image
Author: N/A
License: Public Domain
Source Link: publicdomainvectors.org

Filename: clown_dance-new.gif
Name: Clown Neon Animated Gif
Author: Roger Norton
License: CC BY 2.0
Source Link: Flickr

Filename: Man_dances-new.gif
Author: Elogee FishTruck, N/A
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Source Link: SCP Foundation Wiki
Derivative of:

Name: Vector image of juggler clown
Author: N/A
License: Public Domain
Source Link: publicdomainvectors.org

Filename: clown9-new.png
Name: Colorful jester
Author: N/A
License: Public Domain
Source Link: publicdomainvectors.org

Filename: bouncing_clown-new.gif
Author: Elogee FishTruck, N/A
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Source Link: SCP Foundation Wiki
Derivative of:

Name: Happy clown face vector image
Author: N/A
License: Public Domain
Source Link: publicdomainvectors.org

Filename: 6-new.png
Name: Clown vector illustration
Author: N/A
License: Public Domain
Source Link: publicdomainvectors.org

Filename: blue_wizard-new.gif
Author: Elogee FishTruck, N/A
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Source Link: SCP Foundation Wiki
Derivative of:

Name: Wizard Merlin
Author: N/A
License: Public Domain
Source Link: publicdomainvectors

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
time to go cry to mommy

no really i'm actually sort of scared right now.

good job

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article is both odd, confusing, and mildly disturbing. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: don't bother, they're here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bobble's behind this, ain't he?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Amazing SCP: Truely horrifying being is repelled by a silly bane.

But if you thought that a monster was tracking you, a really real dangerous monster, and that talking like a pirate would keep it away… well you'd do it.

The fact it's an "Interactive" piece because the inbuilt protection fails is just the cherry on top of an already good idea.

Also a neat bit that the clwon isn't the horror, just the random element that creates the limited protection

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: somehow i never saw this. i've stayed at the clown motel several times and i have photographs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't you love farce? My fault, I fear
I thought you wanted what I wanted
Sorry, my dear
But where are the clowns?
Quick, send in the clowns

Isn't it rich? Isn't it queer?
Losing my timing this late
In my career?
And where are the clowns?
There ought to be clowns

Well, maybe next year

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Coming back to this four years later, I'm surprised it hasn't broken +200 yet. It is, in my opinion, one of the best examples of wackyscary we have around here.

EDIT: Much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unrelated kinda, but you mentioning Mr. Laigh made me thing of Laughy McLaugherson. You know, the host of SCP-2020. And now I'm scared shitless by a "Laugh is Fun" episode revolving around clowns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should probably post that list, actually. To make sure the info doesn't get lost.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The inoculations are very creepy. For some reason I can't help but think of the recent clown-attack epidemic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not exactly scared of clowns, but this sends chill down my spine. +1

And why are some people who don't get the reason of format screw? No offense, but the "cute clown" inoculation showed a "FILE NOT FOUND", and that's why the scip starts to corrupt the file… Right?

item.title: Oh... My....... God........
item.content:encoded:
Amazing SCP! Clowns are scary as he-………. That picture….. It seems familiar…….. Let me see the name of it.

" bobble.gif "

IT'S HIM.

item.title: This was on Ghost Adventures.
item.content:encoded:
Series 11, Episode 10.
And it's haunted in real life.
Just watch the episode.
+1 because of the Ham joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Separate. The bobble jpg. was just a little callback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't like "zomgrandom" internet humor any more than anyone else, and yet that's not really the feeling I get from this. It's too… coherently offbeat for that, if that makes any sense. This is something with an actual personality instead of LOLLERCOASTER CHEESE SQUIRRELS MONKEY NINJAS, even if that personality is weird as shit.

Also I laughed at the ham line. I am a mature adult.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Clown Motel exists irl, it's in Tonopah, Nevada and the sign there actually says "Truckers ok" with a thumbs up hand as "ok". Also it's located next to a graveyard. Sweet Dreams…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
molesting a ham

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I talk about this one a lot to my friends still. It's left a lasting impression because it's just so damn strange and honestly a bit chilling. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So is this meant to be an extension of 993, or is it a separate SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*booo-oooop* *booo-oooop*
ATTENTION ALL PERSONNEL. SCP-2170 CONTAMINATION HAS BEEN CONFIRMED THROUGHOUT THE SITE. INFORMATION/MEMETICS QUARANTINE INITIATED. CODE FOSSOR IS NOW IN EFFECT. I REPEAT, CODE FOSSOR IS NOW IN EFFECT. PLEASE CONSULT THE DOCUMENTS "PLEASING CLOWN IMAGES" AND "CONTRIBUTIONS OF CLOWNS TO MODERN SOCIETY" LOCATED IN YOUR EMERGENCY PROTOCOLS HANDBOOK FOR IMMEDIATE INCREASE IN PERSONAL CLOWN APPRECIATION. I REPEAT, SCP-2170 CONTAMINATION CONFIRMED. INFORMATION/MEMETICS QUARANTINE INITIATED. CODE FOSSOR IS NOW IN EFFECT.
*booo-oooop* *booo-oooop*
…
OH GOD DAMN IT I'M THE ONLY ONE HERE WHO ISN'T CREEPED OUT BY CLOWNS, AREN'T I?
*booo-oooop* *booo-oooop*

But seriously, if this is a rapidly spreading memetic that causes actual fatalities and can only be countered by clown appreciation (which seems to be fairly difficult to acquire for most people) then wouldn't this warrant a Keter designation? I mean, if they got loose in a major city (or even just a Foundation site) they could do quite a lot of damage…
Nevertheless, I like that this is a creepy clown SCP where the clowns are, for lack of a better term, innocent. I actually skipped over the inoculations at first because I thought that they were the memetic effect, and the SCP was rewriting the article to expose personnel to them. Fortunately, I believe I possess adequate innate clown appreciation to protect me for long enough to give you a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey kids are ya down with clown?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

:(

I like clowns

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was quite a read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has to be my favorite thing you have ever written sorts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh hey, I've been away from SCP business for a while and it's cool to see we have a whole new batch of stuff and holy crap this is the most horrifyingly surreal thing ever since CLOWN MILK.

My favourite excerpt is this thing near the end of the table:

19██ "molesting a ham will go unpunished." Context unknown NOT VERIFIED, the ham was of age and gave consent

It's… magnificent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to think that the allusions to Bobble are there because the person reading this was part of the research/security staff assigned to 993, and is going through some hallucinatory PTSD because of the entities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have no idea what the hell I just read…

But I think I like it.

item.title: Re: Easter Egg?
item.content:encoded: I was reading on the expectation that there would be a reference. Not much of an easter egg if it's obvious.

item.title: Easter Egg?
item.content:encoded: Did anyone else notice the Bobble the Clown reference at the bottom of the last table?

item.title: I can't understand
item.content:encoded: I am sorry but I am currently unable to comprehend what this is or what it does. It reminds me of today's "Internet humor", with randomness in place of real humor. It also seems to rely on the fear of clowns… Sorry, but unless it gets better (i.e. funnier or eerie-er), I'm going to have to downvote.

item.title: Re: I love this, because clowns are basically modern Shamans,
item.content:encoded:
Clowns as shamans, I love that!

Regarding the symptoms: Back in the 1900s there was a plague in Tonopah that was probably just influenza. But some of the rumors going around about it at the time said that it killed men exclusively, and turned them into swollen, blackened husks within 24 hours. The people who died were apparently buried in the old graveyard that is right next door to the Clown Motel.

The Tonopah Death Plague was probably nothing like what they said it was, but I love alluding to creepy stuff I find when I'm doing research. So I alluded to the idea that unprotected exposure to the entities makes you swell up and burn. Unless you like clowns! But no one likes clowns.

item.title: I love this, because clowns are basically modern Shamans,
item.content:encoded:
…they go into "temporary altered states of consciousness" and attempt to pacify "evil spirits" within humanity, healing, divining, or disillusioning dysfunctional sociological/political/spiritual orthodoxy using primordial hijinks and trickery. Aren't these parasites trying to help us by reminding that Bobble the clown is "educational"? He kind of is, even if you believe his teaching-how-to-make-fire is not canon. They do seem to have the same inscrutable mindfucking role as clowns, maybe the person who wrote that text was hallucinating and bobble is anything but educational?

It actually makes sense that the people who find joy and laughter, instead of fear and paranoia, in those uncanny masqueraded fools are inoculated against these memetic-entity horror hallucinations. The bizarre symptoms are kind of confusing, I guess it has something to do with derealization/empathy weirdness…but I have no idea what the cancerous burning thing is about, unless that text is a hallucination which leads to the self-harm?!?

Since they aren't actually clowns (hence the "random" magickal wizard at the end) they value existence more than benevolence, still pretty creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of an entity that is a reflection of ones ideas of a clown is… interesting. The formatting is in line with what I would expect from a 'true' idea or concept that is anomalous

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorts, I totally love this. Not sure if it was because I'm only just reading it after a few edits, but I was able to figure out the important stuff, and I thought it was all handled well. It was a nice twist on the scary clown trope, balancing a creepy but cliched SCP against some wacky countermeasures that made the format screw really work for me. Excellent, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha, I've driven past the Clown Motel before. Wondered what the hell a place like that was doing out in the middle of nowhere in Nevada - amusing to see this article provide an answer. Anyway, I don't think I have the fear of clowns necessary to make this creepy per se, but I thought it was hilarious and so I upvoted anyway. Great article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit. HOLY SHIT. Look at the last "statement" made by the entities, right before the final inoculation image, and pay close attention to what number the statement was. Oh, he is very educational.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've pretty much finished polishing this one but those aren't bad suggestions, I'll take them into consideration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you could radically improve this one just by changing this line:

There is no direct relation between clowns and SCP-2170 beyond this fact, and SCP-2170 expresses distaste for clown iconography.

to:

SCP-2170 expresses distaste for positive clown-related thoughts and mental imagery.
There is no direct relation between clowns and SCP-2170 beyond this fact.

and making that the opening statement of the description. That way, you just open up with the idea"The clowns are not the SCP! For the love of all that is holy PLEASE just keep TRYING to like clowns or we're all screwed!" before you even get into what these things actually are.

I'd also suggest that instead of having the overwritten text shrink down to the point that its illegible and then followed by the added comments, just ovever watched IT?ctual text with random anttrueown statements. It broke immersion for me, having to stop and copy-paste text into notepad to figure out what it said, when the idea is that its SCtotally not changing the text with its own comments. Wouldn't hurt to have some of the images subtly corrurpted right at the end, as well-maybe a clown in the same cartoony style holding a cleaver- the idea being that the WTF? moment of that one is where it breaks loose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, he might make a good emergency containment procedure: Here's a microphone and a phonebook. Just keep reading out loud and making people notice you're a memetically hilarious clown until we can herd the mind destroying entities back into the hotel…

item.title: Soup
item.content:encoded: That last part broke suspension of disbelief for me. I know for a fact that I make a terrible soup. I've eaten me soup before and, let me tell you, I taste absolutely AWFUL.Too much fat content makes me soup taste greasy. If they know how to make low-calorie me soup, I might change my mind. Downvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only the clowns can save us…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this. I had no problem at all understanding what was going on, and I thought it was great the whole way through. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't get that the format screw was due to the clown inoculation failing; I thought the clown inoculation was a lie and the article had been corrupted, by the thing, for the purpose of getting the thing into your head, and the bit about "only living in this location" was either a lie or something they were trying to escape.

And I thought that was lame, and think the idea of the anti-memetic inoculation failing because nobody likes clowns is amazing, so it's probably a good idea to make it a bit clearer what is going on. If there was something earlier on that talked about how text altered by 2170 exhibits certain trends (in punctuation or what have you), which you then exhibit later in the document, then that makes it clearer we're reading a page that creatures are messing with, and not a page that's a creature messing with us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That means redacted breakfast is real. D: help

Regardless, I feel I should write a tale about this. I could call it "Are We Coul Yet?"

And that's precisely why I won't do it.

Also, congrats on freaking moose out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Vince is right, it was John Gacy. Gacy performed as a clown for parties and charity events, and also stuffed the bodies of over thirty young men into his crawlspace. Didn't do the murders as part of his clown persona though, I bet it could be argued that the clown thing came from a different side of him but I'm not qualified to say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I forget who, but there was a serial killer who was a clown in his day job. Bundy or Gacy, I think.

Also

archives/imagedump/clasd/bobble.gif

NOPE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I may sound like an idiot but what "guy" are you referring to? Are you talking about Pennywise the Dancing Clown or are you talking about an actual clown murderer? (Not someone who murders clowns but a clown who murders people)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It might just be me being a worry-wort, but should there be some kind of warning about all the flashing lights on the last gif? I'm not particularly photosensitive (on good days, at least), so it just hurt my eyes a bit, but it might affect some of our other users in a bad way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad to know someone does. I've always felt sorry for actual clowns who just wanna entertain kids and what a bad rap they get because of some bad artwork and that one guy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's just because it's 4:30 am, but I really fucking dreaded opening that last picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: holy jesus shit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's Seanbaby. It's pretty much always Seanbaby.

Seanbaby's Man Comics are not safe for work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always legitimately enjoyed clowns. Does that make me weird?
But seriously, even so, this was disconcerting as hell in the best possible way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cicero

Jester.

Kefka Palazzo

Jester.

Joker

Homocidal maniac with acid burns.

Nope, you're fucked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not that mean (tho some say otherwise because of 899), in reality I'm just like you with jump scare images.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There have been images on this site that change when you click on them, so I was a little freaked out, ha ha!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if there's an accidental cross-test with Mr. Laugh?
